gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One **content-addressed diagnostic** (§4.3) — the canonical truth. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Present an explicit [`ReasonedError`] as a [`DiagnosticRecord`], optionally shaped by a policy."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The error is returned **unchanged** in [`Presentation::error`] (I1: no policy, level, tag, route,"] # [doc = " or message can cause it not to surface). A policy sets only message / tags / level / route"] # [doc = " (§4.4 I4); the class + site of the refusal are always present, and at [`Level::Minimal`] the"] # [doc = " message names it (I2). The detailed-tier context is allowlist-filtered (X2). When a policy"] # [doc = " applies, the record carries that policy's content hash (`PolicyRef`; §4.4)."] # [must_use] pub fn present (error : ReasonedError , policy : Option < & DiagnosticPolicy >) -> Presentation { let rule = policy . and_then (| p | p . rule_for (& error . class)) ; let message = rule . and_then (| r | r . message . clone ()) . unwrap_or_else (| | error . message . clone ()) ; let level = rule . and_then (| r | r . level) . unwrap_or (Level :: Minimal) ; let tags = rule . map (| r | r . tags . clone ()) . unwrap_or_default () ; let route = rule . and_then (| r | r . route . clone ()) ; let policy_ref = match (policy , rule) { (Some (p) , Some (_)) => Some (p . content_id () . as_str () . to_owned ()) , _ => None , } ; let diagnostic = DiagnosticRecord { class : error . class . as_str () . to_owned () , message , site : error . site . clone () , reason : error . reason . clone () , level , tags , route , context : allowlist (error . context . clone ()) , policy : policy_ref , } ; Presentation { diagnostic , error } }

gaps.snippet:
impl DiagnosticRecord { # [doc = " Resolve this diagnostic's `route` to its RFC-0008 [`SinkBinding`] (M-354, RFC-0013 §8)."] # [doc = " `None` when no route is set; `Some(Err(_))` when the route string is not in the closed v0 set"] # [doc = " (an explicit [`UnknownRoute`], never a silent misroute). This is the **sink-dispatch** point and"] # [doc = " it lives **outside** [`present`] — so routing (or a failed route resolution) can never gate the"] # [doc = " error's propagation (I1): the error has already surfaced unchanged in [`Presentation::error`]."] # [must_use] pub fn sink (& self) -> Option < Result < SinkBinding , UnknownRoute > > { self . route . as_deref () . map (| r | Route :: resolve (r) . map (Route :: binding)) } # [doc = " The **content address** of this diagnostic (§4.3; ADR-003) — a deterministic BLAKE3 over its"] # [doc = " canonical fields. Identity-stable: the same diagnostic content always hashes the same, so the"] # [doc = " human and JSON projections share it (I3)."] # [must_use] pub fn content_id (& self) -> ContentHash { let mut c = Canon :: new () ; c . str ("mycelium.diagnostic.v1") ; c . str (& self . class) ; c . str (& self . message) ; c . str (& self . site) ; c . opt (self . reason . as_deref ()) ; c . str (match self . level { Level :: Minimal => "minimal" , Level :: Medium => "medium" , Level :: Detailed => "detailed" , }) ; c . h . update (& (self . tags . len () as u64) . to_le_bytes ()) ; for t in & self . tags { c . str (t) ; } c . opt (self . route . as_deref ()) ; c . h . update (& (self . context . len () as u64) . to_le_bytes ()) ; for (k , v) in & self . context { c . str (k) ; c . str (v) ; } c . opt (self . policy . as_deref ()) ; c . finish () } # [doc = " The **JSON projection** (§4.3): the lossless, round-trippable machine record, with its"] # [doc = " content `id` embedded. `from_json(to_json(r))` recovers an equal record with an equal"] # [doc = " `content_id` (I3)."] # [must_use] pub fn to_json (& self) -> String { let mut value = serde_json :: to_value (self) . expect ("a diagnostic record serializes") ; if let serde_json :: Value :: Object (map) = & mut value { map . insert ("id" . to_owned () , serde_json :: Value :: String (self . content_id () . as_str () . to_owned ()) ,) ; } serde_json :: to_string (& value) . expect ("a json value serializes") } # [doc = " Recover a record from its JSON projection (I3). The embedded `id` is informational — it is"] # [doc = " recomputed from the recovered fields, so the round-trip is over the semantic content."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns a [`serde_json::Error`] if the input is not a well-formed diagnostic record."] pub fn from_json (s : & str) -> Result < Self , serde_json :: Error > { serde_json :: from_str (s) } # [doc = " The **human projection** (§4.3), graded by [`self.level`](Self::level). Minimal names the"] # [doc = " refusal (class + message + site; I2); medium adds the reason; detailed adds the allowlisted"] # [doc = " context. The content `id` is embedded so the human view carries the same identity as the JSON"] # [doc = " one (I3)."] # [must_use] pub fn to_human (& self) -> String { let mut out = String :: new () ; out . push_str (& format ! ("[{}] {} (at {})" , self . class , self . message , self . site)) ; if let Some (route) = & self . route { out . push_str (& format ! (" → {route}")) ; } if ! self . tags . is_empty () { let tags : Vec < & str > = self . tags . iter () . map (String :: as_str) . collect () ; out . push_str (& format ! ("  #{}" , tags . join (" #"))) ; } if self . level >= Level :: Medium { if let Some (reason) = & self . reason { out . push_str (& format ! ("
  reason: {reason}")) ; } } if self . level >= Level :: Detailed { for (k , v) in & self . context { out . push_str (& format ! ("
  {k}: {v}")) ; } } out . push_str (& format ! ("
  id: {}" , self . content_id () . as_str ())) ; out } }
